enonce.texte:
Read the following text about mobile service packages and answer the questions below.

TELECOM CAMEROON - NEW SERVICE PACKAGES

We are pleased to announce our new range of service packages designed to meet your communication needs:

  • Package A (Basic): 1000 FCFA - 30 minutes of calls + 50 SMS + 500 MB of data (valid for 7 days)
  • Package B (Standard): 2500 FCFA - 100 minutes of calls + 200 SMS + 2 GB of data (valid for 15 days)
  • Package C (Premium): 5000 FCFA - 300 minutes of calls + 500 SMS + 5 GB of data (valid for 30 days)

To subscribe, dial *123# and follow the instructions. For more information, visit our nearest agency or call our customer service at 8000.
images: A simple illustration showing three mobile phone packages with different colors (green for Basic, blue for Standard, gold for Premium) with icons representing calls, SMS, and data.

questions.texte: Identify the package that offers the longest validity period.
questions.reponse: Package C (Premium) offers a validity period of 30 days.

questions.texte: Explain the difference between Package A and Package B in terms of data allowance.
questions.reponse: Package B offers 2 GB of data, which is four times more than Package A's 500 MB (or 0.5 GB).

questions.texte: Justify why a student who needs to make frequent calls to their family might choose Package C.
questions.reponse: A student who needs to make frequent calls would choose Package C because it offers 300 minutes of calls, which is the highest among all packages. This is more economical than subscribing to multiple smaller packages.

questions.texte: Compare the cost per minute of calls between Package A and Package B.
questions.reponse: Package A: 1000 FCFA for 30 minutes = 33.33 FCFA per minute. Package B: 2500 FCFA for 100 minutes = 25 FCFA per minute. Therefore, Package B offers a better value for calls at 25 FCFA per minute compared to 33.33 FCFA per minute for Package A.

enonce.texte:
Your friend Ali wants to subscribe to a mobile service package but doesn't know how to do it. Write a short dialogue between you and Ali where you explain the subscription process using the correct phrasal verbs and expressions of preference.

Use at least three phrasal verbs related to ICTs (e.g., sign up, log in, set up, top up, switch off) and two expressions of preference (e.g., prefer to, would rather, like... better).

questions.texte: Construct a dialogue of at least 8 exchanges between you and Ali about subscribing to a service package.
questions.reponse:
Ali: Hello! I want to subscribe to a mobile package but I don't know how to do it. Can you help me?
Me: Of course! First, you need to sign up for a package that suits your needs. Which one do you prefer?
Ali: I would rather choose a package with more data because I use the internet a lot.
Me: Then you should set up Package C. It offers 5 GB of data. Dial *123# to get started.
Ali: What should I do after that?
Me: Just follow the instructions on your screen. You'll need to log in to your account to confirm the subscription.
Ali: And how do I top up when the package expires?
Me: You can go to any telecom agency or use mobile money. Don't forget to switch off your Wi-Fi to use the mobile data!
Ali: Thanks a lot!
Me: You're welcome!

sousQuestions.texte: List the phrasal verbs you used in the dialogue.
sousQuestions.reponse: The phrasal verbs used are: sign up, set up, log in, top up, switch off.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the expressions of preference you used.
sousQuestions.reponse: The expressions of preference used are: 'Which one do you prefer?' and 'I would rather choose'.

questions.texte: Rewrite the dialogue in reported speech. Start with: 'Ali asked me to help him subscribe to a mobile package...'
questions.reponse: Ali asked me to help him subscribe to a mobile package because he didn't know how to do it. I told him that he first needed to sign up for a package that suited his needs. I asked him which one he preferred. He replied that he would rather choose a package with more data because he used the internet a lot. I suggested that he set up Package C because it offered 5 GB of data. I told him to dial *123# to get started. He asked what he should do after that. I advised him to follow the instructions on his screen and said that he would need to log in to his account to confirm the subscription. He asked how he could top up when the package expired. I explained that he could go to any telecom agency or use mobile money. I reminded him not to forget to switch off his Wi-Fi to use the mobile data. He thanked me and I replied that he was welcome.

enonce.texte:
A telecom company offers two different service packages. Study the information below and help your mother choose the best package for her needs.

Package X: 3000 FCFA - 120 minutes of calls, unlimited SMS, 1.5 GB of data, valid for 10 days

Package Y: 4500 FCFA - 200 minutes of calls, 300 SMS, 3 GB of data, valid for 15 days

Your mother makes about 150 minutes of calls per week, sends about 200 SMS per week, and uses 2 GB of data per week.
images: A split-screen illustration showing Package X on the left with green color and Package Y on the right with orange color, each displaying their respective benefits with icons.

questions.texte: Determine which package is more suitable for your mother. Justify your answer with calculations.
questions.reponse: Your mother needs 150 minutes of calls, 200 SMS, and 2 GB of data per week. With Package X (valid for 10 days ≈ 1.43 weeks), she would need to subscribe twice a month: 2 × 3000 = 6000 FCFA. With Package Y (valid for 15 days ≈ 2.14 weeks), she would need to subscribe about twice a month: 2 × 4500 = 9000 FCFA. Package X is more economical at 6000 FCFA per month compared to 9000 FCFA for Package Y, and it covers her needs with 120 minutes (she needs 150 minutes per week, but can supplement with additional minutes). However, Package Y is more expensive but covers all her needs completely. Therefore, Package X is the more suitable choice considering cost-effectiveness.

questions.texte: Express your mother's preference for the chosen package in a short paragraph. Use at least two expressions of preference.
questions.reponse: My mother prefers Package X because it is more affordable. She would rather choose a package that offers good value for money. She likes Package X better than Package Y because it covers most of her needs at a lower cost.

questions.texte: If the validity period of Package X is extended to 15 days, how would this change your recommendation? Explain.
questions.reponse: If Package X is extended to 15 days, it would become the clear winner. Your mother would need to subscribe twice a month (every 15 days) at 3000 FCFA each, totaling 6000 FCFA per month. This covers her needs for calls, SMS, and data more completely. The recommendation would remain Package X, but the justification would be even stronger because it offers excellent value for money and covers all her needs with its new validity period.

enonce.texte:
You want to subscribe to a data package for your smartphone. Complete the following conversation between you and the customer service agent by filling in the blanks with appropriate expressions of request and preference.

You: Good morning. I would like to (1) ___________ about your data packages.
Agent: Good morning. We have three packages: Daily, Weekly, and Monthly.
You: Could you (2) ___________ me the details of the Weekly package?
Agent: Sure. The Weekly package costs 1500 FCFA and offers 1 GB of data.
You: I (3) ___________ prefer a package with more data. Do you have any other options?
Agent: Yes, we have the Monthly package at 5000 FCFA with 5 GB of data.
You: That sounds good. I would (4) ___________ to subscribe to that one. How can I do it?
Agent: You can dial *456# to subscribe.
You: Thank you for your help. I (5) ___________ if you could also tell me how to check my data balance.
Agent: Of course! You can dial #123# to check your balance.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with appropriate expressions of request and preference.
questions.reponse:
(1) get some information / inquire
(2) give / provide
(3) would / would rather
(4) like / love
(5) was wondering / would like to know

questions.texte: Rewrite the customer service agent's response about the Monthly package in reported speech.
questions.reponse: The customer service agent said that they had the Monthly package at 5000 FCFA with 5 GB of data.

questions.texte: Identify the phrasal verbs used in the conversation and explain their meanings.
questions.reponse: 1. 'Check out' - to examine or look at something. 2. 'Sign up' - to register or subscribe to a service.

enonce.texte: Your uncle tried to subscribe to a service package on his phone but encountered a problem. He received an error message saying 'Subscription failed. Insufficient balance.' He has 2000 FCFA on his phone. He wants to subscribe to a package that costs 2500 FCFA. Write an email to the customer service explaining the problem and requesting assistance.
images: A screenshot of a phone showing an error message: 'Subscription failed. Insufficient balance. Please recharge and try again.' with a sad emoji.

questions.texte: Draft an email to the customer service department describing the problem and asking for a solution.
questions.reponse:
Subject: Problem with Subscription

Dear Customer Service,

I am writing to report a problem I encountered while trying to subscribe to a service package. My name is [Your Uncle's Name] and my phone number is [Phone Number].

I attempted to subscribe to the [Package Name] package which costs 2500 FCFA. However, I received an error message saying 'Subscription failed. Insufficient balance.' I currently have 2000 FCFA on my phone, which is 500 FCFA less than the required amount.

I would like to know if there is any alternative package that I can subscribe to with my current balance. Alternatively, could you advise me on the fastest way to top up my balance so that I can complete the subscription?

I would appreciate your assistance in resolving this issue. Please let me know what steps I should take next.

Thank you for your understanding.

Yours sincerely,
[Your Uncle's Name]

sousQuestions.texte: List the phrasal verbs you used in the email.
sousQuestions.reponse: The phrasal verbs used are: 'top up' (to add money to a phone), 'deal with' (to handle or manage), 'look into' (to investigate).

questions.texte: If the customer service offers your uncle the option to pay the remaining 500 FCFA through mobile money, write a short dialogue between your uncle and the customer service agent where he accepts this offer.
questions.reponse:
Agent: Good morning, Sir. We have received your email. Would you like to pay the remaining 500 FCFA through mobile money?
Uncle: Yes, I would prefer that option. How do I proceed?
Agent: You will receive a payment request on your phone. Just confirm the transaction.
Uncle: That sounds convenient. I'll wait for the payment request.
Agent: Once we receive the confirmation, we will activate your subscription immediately.
Uncle: Thank you for your help. I appreciate your assistance.

enonce.texte:
Read the following instructions for subscribing to a service package and answer the questions below.

HOW TO SUBSCRIBE TO OUR SERVICE PACKAGES

  1. Dial *123# from your phone.
  2. Select 'Subscribe' from the menu.
  3. Choose your preferred package (A, B, or C).
  4. Confirm your selection by pressing 1.
  5. You will receive a confirmation SMS.
  6. Your package will be activated immediately.
images: A step-by-step illustration showing a phone screen with each step of the subscription process: step 1 showing *123#, step 2 showing the menu, step 3 showing package selection, step 4 showing confirmation, step 5 showing an SMS, step 6 showing an activated package.

questions.texte: Identify the second step in the subscription process.
questions.reponse: The second step is to select 'Subscribe' from the menu.

questions.texte: Explain what you should do after selecting your preferred package.
questions.reponse: After selecting your preferred package, you should confirm your selection by pressing 1.

questions.texte: Determine the total number of steps required to complete the subscription process.
questions.reponse: The subscription process requires six steps in total.

questions.texte: Rewrite these instructions in a paragraph form using appropriate linking words.
questions.reponse: To subscribe to our service packages, you first need to dial *123# from your phone. Then, you should select 'Subscribe' from the menu. After that, choose your preferred package (A, B, or C). Next, confirm your selection by pressing 1. Consequently, you will receive a confirmation SMS. Finally, your package will be activated immediately.
